mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeflow
mission.description: Kubeflow is a machine learning toolkit for Kubernetes that provides a comprehensive set of tools for building, deploying, and managing machine learning workflows. It is particularly useful for teams looking to implement scalable and modular AI solutions. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for Kubernetes expertise.

steps.title: Install the Kubeflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kubeflow CLI, which is necessary for managing Kubeflow deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/releases/download/v1.10.0/kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv kubeflow /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kubeflow CLI tarball.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the Kubeflow CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kubeflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch the credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.

steps.title: Install Kubeflow
steps.description:
This step installs Kubeflow using the CLI to deploy the necessary components.

```bash

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

```

> Deploy Kubeflow using the specified version.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Kubeflow deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/autoscaling/autoscaling.yaml

```

> Set up monitoring for Kubeflow.
> Enable autoscaling for your deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kubeflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kubeflow and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kfctl delete -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

gcloud container clusters delete kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Delete the Kubeflow deployment.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubeflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data. This step will upgrade your Kubeflow installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > kubeflow-backup.yaml

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-user-example-com

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade to the latest version of Kubeflow.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl CLI is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl by following the official installation guide.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The user does not have the necessary permissions to create resources in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure your user has the correct IAM roles assigned in GCP.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeflow not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubeflow installation was not successful or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the installation and ensure you are using the correct namespace.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new ML frameworks, improved integration with GCP services.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for older Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.9, ensure to migrate your existing pipelines and configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-user-example-com' showing all pods in 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
